titleInfo.title: Signs from the Unseen Realm: Buddhist Miracle Tales from Early Medieval China

mods.abstract: A study and translation of the Mingxiang ji 冥祥記 by Wang Yan 王琰. Assembled around 490. Only preserved as quotations in medieval collectana (Fayuan zhulin). 27: All of this underscores that the stories compiled by Wang Yan (and many others like him) were collective products in the most literal sense. To be sure, Wang played an authorial role in choosing or altering the wording as well as-perhaps most importantly-in selecting material for inclusion in the first place. But the narratives he incorporated into his text were products of collective mentality and collective memory. They were made and transmitted by narrative communities extending through generational time and across geographic space, both before and after him. One of the things the narratives show us is how such memories were formed and preserved.

titleInfo.title: Kuroda Institute Classics in East Asian Buddhism
